dependencies.description: Switch must be able to reach the RADIUS server via IP (UDP 1812 for authentication, UDP 1813 for accounting)
dependencies.name: RADIUS server reachability

dependencies.description: Requires 'aaa new-model', RADIUS server definition, and AAA method list configuration on the switch
dependencies.name: AAA configuration

dependencies.description: Client-side: endpoint must run an 802.1X supplicant (e.g. Windows native, AnyConnect NAM, wpa_supplicant)
dependencies.name: 802.1X supplicant on endpoint

dependencies.description: VLANs referenced in RADIUS attributes must exist on the switch and be allowed on trunks to the access layer
dependencies.name: VLAN infrastructure for dynamic VLAN assignment

failure_modes.description: Device cannot access the network; port remains in unauthorized state despite supplicant being connected

messages.name: EAPoL-Start
messages.when_sent: When supplicant connects or detects authenticator

messages.description: Authentication result from RADIUS relayed to supplicant
messages.name: EAP-Success/Failure
messages.when_sent: After RADIUS responds with Accept or Reject

purpose: Port-based network access control — authenticates devices before granting network access via EAP over LAN

states.description: Port blocked, no traffic allowed except EAPoL (default state)
states.name: UNAUTHORIZED

states.description: Credentials sent to RADIUS server, waiting for response
states.name: AUTHENTICATING

states.description: Authentication failed, held before allowing retry — indicates bad credentials or config
states.name: HELD

timers.description: Interval between EAP-Request/Identity retransmissions to supplicant
timers.name: tx-period

timers.description: Time to wait after authentication failure before retrying
timers.name: quiet-period
timers.tuning_advice: Lower for faster retry in environments with transient RADIUS issues

timers.description: Time to wait for RADIUS server response
timers.name: server-timeout
timers.tuning_advice: Must be long enough for RADIUS round-trip; too short causes false failures

timers.description: Interval between re-authentication attempts for authorized sessions
timers.name: reauth-period

timers.description: Time to wait for supplicant response
timers.name: supp-timeout
timers.on_expiry: EAP exchange times out for this supplicant

transitions.trigger: EAPoL-Start received from supplicant or authenticator initiates
EAP-Identity received from supplicant, forwarded to RADIUS

transitions.failure_if_stuck: Check RADIUS server reachability and shared secret

transitions.trigger: Re-authentication failed or session timeout or link down
MAB (MAC Authentication Bypass) or guest VLAN assignment (fallback)

notes.note: 802.1X is the most feature-rich on IOS-XE. Supports C3PL (Common Classification Policy Language) with 'authentication' commands, policy-maps for identity. IBN (Identity-Based Networking) with ISE integration. Supports Change of Authorization (CoA) for dynamic policy changes.
IOS-XR has limited 802.1X support — primarily a routing platform. Basic port-based auth available on some platforms.
802.1X requires 'feature dot1x'. Supports single-host, multi-host, multi-auth, and multi-domain modes. MAB supported as fallback. Limited compared to IOS-XE feature set.
